principle: Implement Schema.org structured data using JSON-LD placed in the page <head> — it's independent of HTML nesting, reduces implementation errors, and enables rich snippets that provably increase CTR from search results
explanation: X.seo|schema_json_ld: JSON-LD: placed_in_<head>+independent_of_HTML_nesting(why_preferred); microdata+RDFa_embedded_in_HTML -> high_error_rate; Google_confirmed: rich_snippets_increase_CTR; benefits: stars+price+cooking_time+FAQ_in_SERP+answer_boxes+knowledge_panels; key_types: Article+Product+Recipe+LocalBusiness+FAQ+HowTo+BreadcrumbList; test_with: Google_Rich_Results_Test_tool; NO:skipping_schema_for_pages_with_reviews+recipes+products NO:new_implementations_with_microdata; eg: product_text_listing >> Product_schema_with_rating+price -> SERP_shows_stars -> CTR_increases

principle: Optimize images by writing descriptive alt text, using keyword-rich filenames, and surrounding images with relevant text — images with empty alt attributes and generic filenames are completely invisible to search engines
explanation: X.seo|image_seo: alt_text=primary_signal_for_image_search+required_for_accessibility; surrounding_text: immediately_before+after_image -> crawlers_associate_with_image; filenames: descriptive_keyword_slug(blue-widget-setup.webp) > IMG_9876.jpg; lazy_loading_warning: async_JS_image_loading -> Google_may_miss_images -> use_image_sitemap+proper_lazy_load_implementation; format: WebP_for_faster_load; responsive: <picture>_element_with_multiple_sources; NO:empty_alt_text NO:generic_filenames; eg: <img_alt=''_src='img001.jpg'> >> <img_alt='blue_widget_installation'_src='blue-widget-setup.webp'>

principle: Never target the same keyword on multiple pages — cannibalization splits ranking power between competing pages, causing both to rank lower than a single authoritative page would achieve alone
explanation: X.seo|keyword_cannibalization: problem: multiple_pages_targeting_same_keyword -> compete_with_each_other -> both_rank_lower; detection: site:yourdomain.com_keyword -> see_competing_pages; fix: consolidate_into_one_authoritative_page OR differentiate_each_page_with_distinct_keyword_cluster; rule: each_page_owns_one_primary_keyword_cluster; applies_to: title_tags+H1+main_body; NO:same_keyword_in_multiple_page_titles NO:10_product_variations_all_optimized_for_same_term; eg: /blue-widgets+/blue-widget-shop+/buy-blue-widgets_all_targeting_same_keyword >> single_/blue-widgets -> consolidated_ranking_power

principle: Write semantically comprehensive content covering related topics and synonyms rather than repeating a keyword — keyword density is an obsolete metric and stuffing actively hurts rankings by degrading user experience
explanation: X.seo|content_comprehensiveness: keyword_density=obsolete; semantically_rich: cover_related_concepts+synonyms+sub-topics_user_expects; page_layout: keywords_in_main_body >> keywords_in_sidebar+footer(lower_weight); link_to_relevant_internal+external_resources -> signals_comprehensive_answer; user_satisfaction: comprehensive_page -> lower_bounce -> positive_ranking_signal; NO:repeating_main_keyword_every_100_words NO:tracking_keyword_density_as_KPI; eg: diabetes_page_with_word_diabetes_50x >> page_covering_symptoms+diet+medications+research+lifestyle -> higher_rankings

principle: Use rel=canonical to specify the preferred URL when multiple URLs contain the same content — without it, search engines choose the canonical themselves and consolidate link equity to the page you didn't intend
explanation: X.seo|canonical_tag: use_when: multiple_URLs_for_same_content(www+non-www+https+http+sort_params+session_IDs+print_versions); implementation: <link_rel='canonical'_href='preferred_URL'>_in_<head>; syndication: get_republisher_to_add_canonical_pointing_back_to_original -> all_link_equity_flows_to_original; failure: no_canonical -> Google_picks_canonical_itself(may_choose_wrong_version); forbidden: conflicting_canonicals(A_points_B+B_points_A) OR canonical_on_robots.txt_blocked_page; NO:no_canonical_on_parameter_URLs NO:conflicting_canonical_chains; eg: /products?sort=price+/products?sort=name >> both_canonical=/products -> one_strong_ranking_page
